cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking about the student's description of the illness. The options are (A) not correct, (B) not satisfying, (C) completely discouraging, or (D) accepted. Looking back at the story, the professor was very strict and usually not pleased with students' answers. The student in the story was nervous because he knew the exam would be hard. When the professor asked him to describe a certain illness, the student's description "turned out to be OK." Then the professor asked about the cure, and the student answered "just as right." The key part here is that the student's description of the illness was "OK," which suggests that it was acceptable or correct. The professor didn't seem to have any issue with it at that point. The problem came later when the student gave the dosage as a full spoon instead of five drops. The professor then told him it was too late because the patient had died.
